family.reason: Sandy beaches like Golden Beach and Astris, safe roads, easy ferry access

couples.reason: West coast sunsets, quiet coves, mountain village tavernas

nightlife.reason: Some bars in Limenas and Potos, but no club scene

food.reason: Thassos honey, smoked meats, wild mushrooms, fresh seafood

beaches.reason: Marble Beach, Golden Beach, Aliki, Giola — exceptional variety

walking.reason: Ancient walls walk, Kazaviti path, forest trails in the interior

quick_summary.en: Greece's greenest island — pine forests meet marble-white beaches, mountain villages with fire-walking rituals, and an ancient agora in the centre of the capital.
quick_summary.el: Το πιο πράσινο νησί της Ελλάδας — πευκοδάση συναντούν μαρμάρινες παραλίες, ορεινά χωριά με έθιμο αναστενάριων, και αρχαία αγορά στο κέντρο της πρωτεύουσας.

pros: Exceptional water clarity — marble substrate creates extraordinary colours
Varied beaches — from white marble coves to long golden sands
Mountain villages with genuine character and excellent food
Ancient history embedded in the capital — agora, theatre, walls
Accessible from Kavala — under 1 hour ferry
Good road infrastructure — 95km coastal circuit road
cons: Limited nightlife — not a party destination
Requires car or scooter for full exploration
Few luxury or five-star hotel options
Can feel quiet for those seeking resort-style entertainment
insider_tips: Take the Keramoti ferry from Thessaloniki — shorter crossing and a dramatic arrival
Eat in Theologos village at least once — the lamb and smoked meats are outstanding
Visit the ancient agora at 8am before the heat and crowds
Giola lagoon is best before 9am or after 6pm in summer
Drive the 95km coastal circuit over two days, stopping at every beach and village
Walk the path between Megalo and Mikro Kazaviti — 20 minutes through beautiful forest
mistakes_to_avoid: Not renting a car — you will miss the west coast and mountain villages
Staying only on the east coast — the west coast sunsets are essential
Skipping Theologos and the mountain villages
Expecting a party island — Thassos is relaxed and family-oriented
Not trying Thassos pine honey before leaving
